candidates.why: Represents the company’s primary operating revenues; component of total revenue and appropriate as a top-line revenue measure.
Includes core revenues plus other revenues; best single consolidated top-line measure, so used as highest-priority revenue line.
This is a cost line (COGS), not revenue; should not be used for REVT.
Finance income, not operating revenue; inappropriate for REVT.
Interest income, not operating revenue; inappropriate for REVT.
Bottom-line comprehensive income, not revenue; inappropriate for REVT.

variables.notes: Both 'Revenues' and 'Other revenues' roll into 'Total revenue'. Per instructions, revenue can change label over time, so 'Total revenue' is primary with 'Revenues' as fallback.

candidates.why: Direct costs of materials used in production; part of cost of sales and appropriate for COGS, especially for earlier years before 'Cost of goods sold' appears populated.
'Cost of goods sold' is the standard direct cost of sales line, matching the COGS definition.
These appear to be operating payroll (likely SG&A) rather than direct cost of sales, so not classified as COGS.
General operating overhead, not clearly direct production cost; should be treated as SG&A, not COGS.
R&D is a separate operating expense category, not COGS.
Government grants offset R&D, not a cost of sales line.
Overhead-type operating expense, not direct COGS.
Residual operating overhead bucket; should be treated as SG&A, not COGS.
Mixed other operating item; not clearly COGS.
Financing costs, not cost of sales.
Financing-related, not COGS.
Supplemental R&D disclosure, not COGS.
Balance sheet stock of raw materials, not an income statement flow; not used directly as COGS.

variables.notes: Direct production costs are captured by 'Cost of goods sold' for recent years and 'Cost of materials' for earlier periods; no need to include overhead or residual operating expense buckets in COGS.

candidates.why: Explicitly identified as stock-based compensation within SG&A; a classic SG&A component.
Represents operating overhead not in COGS; typical SG&A bucket.
Mixed other operating item; likely part of SG&A/overhead rather than COGS.

variables.notes: REVIEWED. SG&A should capture overhead components. Here, 'Payroll expenses' (appearing twice but with identical labels) is a major SG&A line even though it is not in the candidate list, so it was added from the full sheet. 'Other operating expenses' and 'Other operating expenses/income - Other' are overhead-type operating expenses, and 'Stock-based Compensation in SGA' is explicitly part of SG&A. Only one instance of each duplicated label is selected due to the no-duplicate-label rule. Manual review recommended to confirm classification of 'Payroll expenses' and the mixed 'Other operating expenses/income - Other' as SG&A rather than partly COGS. | Escape-hatch used (final_choice outside candidate list): [Income Statement] Payroll expenses

candidates.why: Core R&D expense line; matches the R&D definition and is the primary measure of R&D spending.
Represents government grants offsetting R&D, not the gross R&D expense itself; better treated as an adjustment rather than the main XRD line.
Supplemental disclosure of R&D, but the main recognized expense is in 'Research and Development Expense'.

variables.notes: The main R&D expense line is used as XRD. Government grants and supplemental disclosures are not chosen as separate primary XRD measures.

candidates.why: Primary interest expense line; best representation of financing costs for XINT.
Interest expense related to lease liabilities; a subset of total interest expense and useful as a fallback if the main interest line is missing.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental
candidates.why: Supplemental disclosure of lease interest; can serve as an additional fallback measure of interest expense.
Balance sheet liability, not an expense line; not appropriate for XINT.
Balance sheet liability, not an expense line; not appropriate for XINT.

final_choice.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental

variables.notes: XINT is mapped to the main 'Interest expenses' line, with lease-related interest lines provided as lower-priority fallbacks for periods where the main line may be missing or aggregated differently.

candidates.why: Represents total shareholders’ equity; no separate parent vs non-controlling interest line is present, so this is the best proxy for book equity attributable to owners.
Duplicate capitalization of 'Total equity' with identical numbers; same concept but redundant as a second BE line.
Balancing/plug line, not a primary reported equity figure; should be avoided when a proper total equity line exists.
Balancing/plug line, not the main equity figure; avoid in favor of 'Total equity'.

candidates.row_label: Balancing Item - Total Liability and Equity
candidates.why: Technical balancing row, not an equity measure itself.
Represents total assets or total capital structure, not equity alone; inappropriate for BE.

variables.notes: No separate non-controlling interest line exists, so 'Total equity' is used as book equity. The alternative 'Total Equity' row is a duplicate presentation and is not needed as an additional BE line.
No balance sheet line for non-controlling interests or minority interests appears in the provided data, so MIB is set to an empty list (treated as 0).
